my_thoughts: The subject_object_inversion mechanism works: re-asks root question ('Then who is the witness?'), forces identity assertion (must be asserted by student, not located as object). The student's achieving conceptual clarity (conceptual clarity, rejection of objectification) shows they understand: witness = not objectifiable (cannot be found as object, must be asserted as subject). They'll likely assert 'I am' next. Hypothesis: forcing identity assertion after conceptual clarity produces self-identification—testable by tracking whether students assert 'I am' after recognizing witness cannot be objectified.
accumulated_insight: Six-turn pattern: T1: Student feels blocked ('The inner teacher is not easily reached')—constraint collapse asserts immediacy. T2: Student looks within, finds content ('sensations, perceptions, thoughts...')—ontological shift distinguishes self from content. T3: Student says 'I find it escapes me'—source tracing questions location. T4: Student locates in mind ('In the mind')—subject object inversion escalates ('who knows the mind?'). T5: Student recites doctrine ('The witness of the mind knows the mind')—ground reality check tests validity. T6: Student achieves clarity ('Of course not. He would have been just another idea in the mind')—achieving conceptual clarity via conceptual clarity, rejection of objectification. The subject_object_inversion mechanism re-asks root question ('Then who is the witness?'), forces identity assertion. Pattern: difficulty → content → escape → location → doctrine → clarity → [next: identity assertion]. They've shifted: witness = not objectifiable (conceptual clarity).
